review_1_open_observations: P1-9 scope named bookticker status.json, but implementer left it unchanged, judging line 518 a historical note inside an already-accepted record (status=accepted, user_acceptance=accepted_merged_and_pushed) and that rewriting a status.json note risks tampering with audit evidence. Bookkeeper did not self-fix; review-1 should adjudicate whether the residual 'remain' wording needs normalization or is legitimate history.

RESOLVED BY REVIEW-2 (F3): review-1 accepted leaving bookticker status.json untouched, but review-2 (final gate) overruled it — status.json:518,653 still carry future/pending wording contradicting the accepted state, and must be historicized (past tense / time-scoped) while preserving every fact. The fix round therefore edits bookticker status.json. Review-2 authority prevails.

review_1.reviewer_prior_involvement_notes: Kimi was one of the four read-only audit models that informed the backlog; did not author design/breakdown/implementation/fix. Provider (Moonshot) is isolated from implementer claude_glm (Zhipu GLM).
review_1.p1_9_adjudication: Review-1 accepts leaving bookticker status.json untouched as a frozen accepted-stage audit record (no contradictory tokens present; rewriting notes risks evidence tampering). Living-doc normalization satisfied via handoff + implementation reports.

review_2.report_capture: Codex ran read-only codex exec (no file write); bookkeeper recovered raw output verbatim from Codex rollout session 019f6a94-9685-7f80-8b64-cb7ba5798daf and landed 50-review-2.md. Exercises RC9.
findings_summary: F1 (P1): contract funding-history 'empty proves upstream fetch' is false vs snapshot_service pure PublishedState projection; document empty!=success, disclose funding-history.schema.json:34 stale prose as deferred schema-alignment.

F2 (P1): contract symbol-snapshot ok/partial/timeout enum described narrower than code (premium/history failures also partial; no-worker/no-new-publication also timeout); align to code, warnings carries reasons; disclose symbol-snapshot.schema.json:39 without changing schema.

F3 (P1): P1-9 normalization incomplete — bookticker status.json:518,653 + 70-handoff:63-66,129 + 20-implementation:79-82 still future/pending; historicize preserving all facts. OVERRULES review-1's leave-status.json decision.
review_2.reviewer_prior_involvement_notes: Honest disclosure: Codex (openai) participated in the read-only four-model audit that produced the backlog (direction-level input). It did NOT author the stage design, development breakdown, implementation, or any fix. Provider isolation holds on every hard/preferred axis: Codex (openai) differs from the implementer claude_glm (zhipu_glm) and from the designer+breakdown author (Anthropic Opus). Because reviewer provider != designer provider, the validator's designer-overlap evidence-file requirement (validate-stage.py:720-737) does NOT apply; no strong-reviewer override is invoked.

isolation_analysis.vs_direction_audit: NOT isolated — Codex was one of four read-only audit models; disclosed; dispatch requires independent re-verification against PRD/schema/code truth, not an echo of its own audit

design_conflict_override.reason: Not needed: reviewer provider (openai) does not overlap the designer/breakdown-author provider (anthropic), so validate-stage.py's designer-overlap path is not triggered. The direction-audit participation is disclosed but does not require the override machinery.

options: re-review Task C and recompute review-1 fingerprint
encode user-authorized fast-route exception in Harness
knowingly accept as one-off historical exception and record it
linked_decisions.resolution: Resolved via Stage A (RC4). After task-level fingerprint coverage + authorized_exception land, bookticker goes green through (a) per-task coverage or (b) recorded exemption; no hand-edit of historical fingerprints, no long-lived red gate. See 81 Q4.

ADOPT, per Fable5 verdict (81-harness-design-rootcause-review-fable5.md). Two batches, template-repo first. Mandatory condition R1: every new pre-accept assertion must carry an authorized_exception + evidence-file slot, reusing validate-stage.py:735-737 override pattern, or it mass-copies the RC4 permanent-false-red defect.

landing_sequence.stage_A_template_first: RC4: task-level fingerprint coverage + authorized_exception mechanism (shared infra for all doc gates). Small diff, focused evidence-integrity review. Also resolves D-A.
landing_sequence.stage_B_reuses_exemption: Doc gates: RC1(a) pending_promotions[] ledger (contains RC1(b) as its trigger); RC2 generation of STAGE_INDEX from status.json (RC7 manifest classification as prerequisite; sidecar for human Note column; pre-accept thin assertion 'regenerator diff is empty'); RC3(a) living-doc token normalization.
landing_sequence.batch_2_P1: RC5(b) check-dangling-refs.py; RC3(a) with R1 exemption slot.
landing_sequence.one_time_backfill: Regenerate STAGE_INDEX (5 missing + 2 dangling per RC8) and half-generate ROADMAP Done after Stage B gates exist.
landing_sequence.two_repo_sync: Template repo edits validator/schema -> manual cp downstream (no auto sync) -> next in-repo stage tests new gates. Do not overwrite AGENTS.md two-repo carve-out lines.

fable5_review.verdict: ACCEPT (direction and evidence hold); corrections F1/F2/F4/F5 required before report serves as D-B basis; cross-cutting R1 mandatory.
report_corrections_applied: F1 RC2 citation (STAGE_INDEX obligation is in STAGE_INDEX.md header, not AGENTS.md)
F4 harness-design.md has no Status date line at all
F5 symbol-snapshot 2 passing mentions vs funding-history 0
